\noindent The Shapiro-Wilk normality test is executed
through the \ttblue{shapiro.test} function from
the \ttblue{stats} package.

\par\addvspace{\topsep}
\begingroup
\fvset{listparameters={%
  \setlength{\topsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\partopsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\parsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\itemsep}{0pt}%
}}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_blue}]
shapiro_stat, shapiro_p = 
shapiro_test(ussteamco_mod_3.resid)
print({'W': shapiro_stat, 'p_value': shapiro_p})
\end{Verbatim}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_light_blue}]

	Shapiro-Wilk normality test

data:  ussteamco.mod.3$residuals
W = 0.948, p-value = 0.09045
